name: dup-invoice-race
regression_for: v38.182.0 — atomic Postgres SEQUENCE next_invoice_no() replaces the racy Master sheet RPC counter (`getNextInvoiceId`).
summary: On 2026-05-02 17:57:41 + 17:57:42 UTC two concurrent createInvoice calls (Nip Tuck NORTON + Nip Tuck NIPTUCK, ~1.18s apart) both observed the Master Price List sheet's invoice counter at the same value, both incremented, both wrote the same number — INV-000131 — onto separate invoices. The customer received two PDFs containing 18 overlapping NIPTUCK line items totaling $450 that should have been on one invoice. Two cousin incidents bit other clients earlier (Digs INV-000115, Mary Kenngott INV-000129). The 2026-05-04 fix (v38.182) replaced the racy Master sheet RPC counter with a Postgres atomic SEQUENCE `public.invoice_no_seq` accessed via `next_invoice_no()`. This fixture asserts that the SB-side createInvoice rewrite continues to use the SEQUENCE and produces strictly-monotonic, unique invoice numbers — never reaching back to the racy counter that's still in place for non-invoice numbering (e.g., shipment numbers).

cases.name: single-call-uses-sequence
cases.description: A single createInvoice call must allocate its invoice number by advancing public.invoice_no_seq exactly once via next_invoice_no(). The returned invoice_no must equal the new sequence value. This proves the handler does not reach back to the legacy Master sheet RPC counter (`getNextInvoiceId`) — that call site MUST stay only for non-invoice counters per CLAUDE.md 'never regress the v38.182 atomic invoice-number counter' rule.

cases.name: two-consecutive-calls-produce-distinct-numbers
cases.description: Two consecutive single-row invoice creates (each using a separate ledger row from preState — see test_isolation note below) MUST produce strictly-different invoice_no values. The atomic SEQUENCE guarantees this at the SQL level; the assertion is here so a future refactor that accidentally reaches back to a racy read-then-write counter regresses loudly. This is the regression catch for the original incident: the two real-world calls on 2026-05-02 both produced INV-000131.
cases.test_isolation_note: Harness must reset preState between consecutive cases OR allocate fresh ledger row IDs per call. Recommended: harness uses two distinct ledger rows from preState (RCVG-FIXTURE-001-1 for call A, RCVG-FIXTURE-001-2 for call B) so neither invocation sees an already-Invoiced row.

harness_implementation_notes: The 'invoice_no_uses_sequence' assertion is implemented as: peek before call → invoke handler → peek after call → assert (after - before) == sequence_advanced_by AND returned invoice_no's numeric portion == after.

case 'two-consecutive-calls-produce-distinct-numbers' uses input_a/input_b instead of a single 'input' field; the schema in supabase/parity-fixtures/README.md may need a small extension to formalize cross-call cases. P1.7 is the natural place for that extension.

links.build_status_section: Recent Changes (2026-05-04, atomic invoice counter); also 'Recent Changes (2026-05-05, dup-number race incident — triage, cleanup, hardening backlog)'
links.migration_decision: MIG-005 references the atomic counter as a do-not-regress pin
links.claude_md_rule: Never regress the v38.182 atomic invoice-number counter. next_invoice_no() Postgres SEQUENCE is the only thing standing between the system and the dup-number race repeating.
